mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month growth in retail sales reflects strengthening household demand and near-term revenue momentum, causing higher S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when RSAFS growth is above its recent trend.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value was 373946.0 in the point-in-time macro snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than lower RSAFS growth in walk-forward tests, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% combined with a recent increase tightens financial conditions and raises equity discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days after the threshold is crossed.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5% and is higher than 3 months earlier
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 5.25 in the point-in-time macro snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive mean 20-day S&P 500 returns or if negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of triggered instances in walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth paired with a falling unemployment rate confirms broad labor-market income growth, raising expected corporate revenues and causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises over 3 months and UNRATE falls at least 0.2pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest available value was 138038.0 and UNRATE was 4.5 in the point-in-time macro snapshot
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down signal does not outperform signals using PAYEMS alone or UNRATE alone, or if triggered 60-day returns are not positive on average.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production signals weakening real activity and lower operating leverage for cyclical firms, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when INDPRO loses momentum.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average after being above it in the prior month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value was 112.6889 in the point-in-time macro snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO downside-crossing events are followed by positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns or if the downside signal has a nonnegative walk-forward coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp fall in housing starts reduces construction activity, durable-goods demand, and credit-sensitive household wealth effects, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 10% over 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value was 1474.0 in the point-in-time macro snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST declines greater than 10% over 6 months are followed by nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if hit-rate for negative returns is below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPI with an already high federal funds rate signals restrictive policy risk and margin pressure from inflation, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds its 12-month growth and FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value was 207.387 and FEDFUNDS was 5.25 in the point-in-time macro snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the CPI-acceleration and high-FEDFUNDS joint trigger does not predict lower 20-day returns than either CPIAUCSL acceleration alone or FEDFUNDS above 4.5% alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as risk premia reprice.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value was 4.5 in the point-in-time macro snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE increases greater than 0.3pp over 3 months are followed by positive average 5-day S&P 500 returns or if the signal has negative-return hit-rate below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous gains in industrial production and retail sales identify a synchronized real-activity upswing, raising earnings expectations and causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both increase over the most recent 3-month window
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value was 112.6889 and RSAFS was 373946.0 in the point-in-time macro snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if joint INDPRO-up and RSAFS-up observations do not produce higher average 5-day S&P 500 returns than the unconditional sample or if either single-series signal fully subsumes the joint effect.
